How to use Playwright

Playwright is a Node.js library for browser automation. It supports Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit. In this guide we cover installation, the API, and common patterns. The library is maintained by Microsoft and has become the de facto standard for end-to-end testing of modern web applications. Installation is straightforward: npm install playwright. Once installed, you can write tests against any of the supported browsers using a unified API surface.

The persistent context API is particularly useful when you need to retain cookies and local storage between sessions. This is the foundation of long-lived scraping setups and any automation that needs to behave like a returning user rather than a fresh visitor each time.
